Detective Cupp and FBI Agent Park were a brilliant combination, i loved every second of their interactions. I generally love a whodunnit, and this was actually really well written and FUN. So much fun. I usually avoid shows that come out in the last 5 to 10 years, and especially the ones produced by Netflix, cause they all follow a specific formula that doesn’t really appeal to me, but im so so so glad i gave this one a chance. Brilliant!! And most importantly: the person who i wanted to be the murderer actually was, which is a nice little bonus for me personally. AND i actually didn’t think it could even be them, and that’s even more impressive of the show writers.

Funny nod having Julian McMahon play the Australian prime minster, given the fact his dad was a former Australian prime minister and Kylie Minogue was his ex-sister in law. 

The whole plot reminded me of an Agatha Christie Poirot episode - Murder on the links (season 6, episode 3) especially in relation to Bruce and Elsyie motives and then the real killer (not trying to spoil) using the disconnect between staff and family in the house to their advantage just like in that poirot plot. 

What I want to know is did Vuis (the young detective) ever get his tour and a bonus to meet the president for his assistance? 

Did Bruce and Elsyie ever take that trip to see the pantheon? Also, what actually happened to the calligrapher?
